records.section: Section 1 — Deciding Which Tools Need Qualification
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2, have you worked out which tools need qualification:
records.query: Have you identified every tool whose output you rely on without independently checking it, so a tool error could put a fault into your software or let one slip past your verification?

records.section: Section 1 — Deciding Which Tools Need Qualification
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2, have you worked out which tools need qualification:
records.query: For each such tool, have you decided whether it is a development tool (its output ends up in the airborne software) or a verification tool (it could fail to catch an error)?

records.section: Section 1 — Deciding Which Tools Need Qualification
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2, have you worked out which tools need qualification:
records.query: Have you assigned each tool a Tool Qualification Level (TQL) based on how it is used and your software level?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2 (and, for DO-178C projects, DO-330):
records.query: Do you have a plan describing how each tool will be qualified, at a rigor matching its TQL?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2 (and, for DO-178C projects, DO-330):
records.query: Does the plan capture the tool operational requirements — what the tool must do, in your environment, for you to trust it?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2 (and, for DO-178C projects, DO-330):
records.query: Have you agreed the qualification approach with the certification authority (for example, stated it in your PSAC)?

records.section: Section 3 — Building the Tool Qualification Data
records.subsection: For DO-178C projects, DO-330 sets the tool life-cycle data:
records.query: For higher-TQL tools, do you have the tool requirements, design, and configuration data your plan calls for?

records.section: Section 3 — Building the Tool Qualification Data
records.subsection: For DO-178C projects, DO-330 sets the tool life-cycle data:
records.query: Are the standards used to develop or configure the tool identified?

records.section: Section 4 — Showing the Tool Actually Works
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2 (and DO-330 for DO-178C projects):
records.query: Have you verified the tool against its operational requirements — that it does what you need, in your real environment?

records.section: Section 4 — Showing the Tool Actually Works
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2 (and DO-330 for DO-178C projects):
records.query: For higher-TQL tools, have you verified the tool's own requirements, design, and code to the extent required?

records.section: Section 4 — Showing the Tool Actually Works
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.2 (and DO-330 for DO-178C projects):
records.query: Do the results show every planned qualification case was run and passed, with any failures resolved?

records.section: Section 5 — Controlling and Summarizing the Tool
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7 and §12.2 (and DO-330 for DO-178C projects):
records.query: Is the tool under configuration management — a known version, with its environment recorded so a result can be reproduced later?

records.section: Section 5 — Controlling and Summarizing the Tool
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7 and §12.2 (and DO-330 for DO-178C projects):
records.query: Is there quality-assurance oversight of the tool qualification activity?

records.section: Section 5 — Controlling and Summarizing the Tool
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7 and §12.2 (and DO-330 for DO-178C projects):
records.query: Do you have a tool configuration index and a summary that record what was qualified and how it meets its objectives?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available from AeroCert. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
